description: Stamp schema_version onto block data on the write path (block-api.ts:870-938, 1758-1767) and admit a single optional top-level schema_version on block schema envelopes, so the read-time AJV hook (block-api.ts:785-789) and the version-mismatch migration branch (schema-validator.ts:225-239) become active for all blocks. The stamp location is the block envelope (top-level schema_version); per-item stamping is out of scope (block-api.ts:782-784).
acceptance_criteria: Envelope schema admits the version: every block schema's top-level envelope declares an optional `schema_version` string property; the relaxation is that one named property only — `additionalProperties:false` is NOT opened wholesale; per-item `additionalProperties:false` is unchanged (no per-item `schema_version`).

Write path produces the envelope version: the write path (writeTypedFile / writeBlockForDir, block-api.ts:870-938, 1758-1767) sets the top-level `schema_version` on the whole-block object to the current schema `version` on every block write; it is stamped, not merely passed through.

Read hook fires on the envelope: with the envelope `schema_version` present, the read-time hook (block-api.ts:785-789) no longer short-circuits on its absence and routes the whole block through validateBlockWithMigrationForDir; demonstrated by a real read-block that exercises the hook, not a mocked assertion.

Version-gap behavior is the two explicit branches — migrate AND throw — on both read and write gates: when the block's envelope `schema_version` differs from the schema `version`, (i) with a registered migration runMigrations walks the whole block forward and validation passes (schema-validator.ts:232-238); (ii) with NO registered migration the read/write gate throws (schema-validator.ts:234-236). Both branches are tested at both the read gate (block-api.ts:785-789) and the write gate (block-api.ts:1758-1767).

The new field reaches already-installed substrates, not only the samples catalog: the envelope `schema_version` property is added to the installed schema bodies of existing substrates via the install/update path, not only to samples/schemas/; an already-installed substrate's blocks carry and validate the version after update.
notes: Stamp location is the block envelope (top-level schema_version), derived: the read/write hooks already key on envelope.schema_version (block-api.ts:785-789, 1758-1767). Not a deferred DECISION.
